title: Quiz: Migration Urgency
description: Test your understanding of PQC migration urgency and business case development

questions.question: What is the primary reason PQC migration must start NOW rather than when quantum computers arrive?

options.text: HNDL attacks are capturing data today for future decryption
It's cheaper to migrate during slow business periods

questions.explanation: Harvest Now, Decrypt Later (HNDL) attacks are actively capturing encrypted data today. This data will be decrypted when quantum computers arrive. You cannot protect data retroactively once it's been captured.

questions.question: How does the cost of PQC migration change with delay?

options.text: Costs stay constant regardless of timing
Costs only matter if quantum computers are built

questions.explanation: Delayed migrations become emergency migrations with compressed timelines, leading to 2-5x higher costs, more errors, and increased risk. Early, planned migrations are far more cost-effective.

questions.question: What is the best response to 'Quantum computers are still years away'?

options.text: Agree and wait for more certainty
HNDL attacks are happening NOW; data captured today will be decrypted later
We should focus on other security priorities
The timeline is too uncertain to act

questions.explanation: The key counter-argument is that adversaries are capturing encrypted data TODAY. The quantum threat isn't just about when CRQC arrives—it's about protecting data that's being harvested right now.

questions.question: What should be migrated FIRST in a PQC transition?

options.text: TLS for sensitive external communications and certificate systems

questions.explanation: Highest priority goes to systems with long-lived secrets AND external exposure: TLS for sensitive external communications, certificate signing systems, and long-term data encryption.

questions.question: NIST post-quantum standards are still in draft form, so organizations should wait before beginning migration.
questions.explanation: False. NIST finalized ML-KEM (Kyber) and ML-DSA (Dilithium) standards in August 2024. There is no longer a technical excuse for waiting—standards are ready and implementations are available.

questions.question: When communicating PQC urgency to executives, what framing is most effective?

options.text: Strategic risk management and competitive advantage

questions.explanation: Executives respond best to strategic framing: risk management, competitive advantage, and regulatory compliance. Fear-based messaging causes paralysis, and technical details aren't decision-relevant.

questions.question: What is a realistic Phase 1 investment for PQC assessment and planning?

options.text: No investment needed—it's just a configuration change

questions.explanation: A realistic Phase 1 (assessment and planning) investment is $200K-500K over 6 months. This includes cryptographic inventory, risk assessment, vendor evaluation, and roadmap development.

questions.question: Which actions can start in Week 1 of a PQC initiative? (Select all that apply)

options.text: Complete full migration of all systems

questions.explanation: In Week 1, you can form a task force (a), brief executives (c), and begin inventory (d). Complete migration (b) takes years and cannot happen in Week 1.
